free_text: Excitation functions of fusion reactions and neutron
transfer in the interaction of 6He with 197Au and 206Pb
A beam of accelerated 6He ions with an energy of up to
about 10MeV/A was used. The beam profile and intensity
were measured with a specially designed multi-wire
proportional diagnostic chamber situated immediately in
Run 1:a stack consisting of one 50 mu-m and twelve 13.5
mu-m thick gold foils, and further downstream -a second
stack of six 206Pb targets, 600-700 mu-g/cm2 each on ~
1.5 mu-m Mylar backing, with 20 mu-m thick Al beam
energy degraders in between the targets.
In Run 2 only a stack of fifteen Au (seven 13.5 mu-m,
four 5 mu-m and four 4 mu-m) targets was irradiated.
In Run 3, the initial energy of the extracted 6He beam
was lowered to 25.5MeV by means of two foils: a gold
one, which simultaneously served as a target for the
excitation function on 197Au, and an Al one. A stack of
seven thin 206Pb targets on havar backings (also
playing the role of energy degraders) was used. After
it, two 5 mu-m 197Au targets were placed in order to
measure the transfer reactions deeply below (Bc~20 MeV)
the Coulomb barrier of the reaction.
In Run 4, the initial energy of the 6He beam was
reduced to 23.3MeV. A 197Au target stack, consisting
of 7 foils (each 6.6 mu-m thick), was placed on the
focal plane of the magnetic spectrometer MSP-144 at
the position of the maximum beam intensity. The beam
dose on the stack in this run was measured by a
induced in the individual gold foils was measured
off-line using four energy and efficiency-calibrated
HPGe detectors (the efficiency was about 4-5% for
